positioning_statement_long: This application provides general wellness and food-comparison information for informational and educational purposes only. It is not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. It is not a substitute for consultation with a registered medical practitioner, registered dietitian, or AYUSH practitioner. No content on this app should be read as a claim to cure, treat, mitigate, or prevent any disease. All suggestions are lifestyle suggestions, not prescriptions.

regulatory_regimes_in_scope.citation: FSS (Health Supplements, Nutraceuticals, Food for Special Dietary Use, Food for Special Medical Purpose, Functional Food, and Novel Food) Regulations, 2022
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.applicability_to_app: The app references supplements such as NMN, resveratrol, KSM-66 ashwagandha, creatine, magnesium glycinate, omega-3, and D3+K2. NMN is not listed in FSSAI Schedule VII / permitted nutraceutical ingredient lists; products sold as NMN in India are regulatorily ambiguous. Other ingredients are permitted subject to dosage limits.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.required_disclaimer_text: Supplements mentioned in this app are food-category products regulated by the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) under the Nutraceutical Regulations, 2022. They are not medicines. We do not prescribe, sell, or dispense supplements. NMN (nicotinamide mononucleotide) is not currently listed as a permitted nutraceutical ingredient under FSSAI Schedule VII; products marketed as NMN in India are of ambiguous legal status and we do not recommend their purchase. Always check the FSSAI licence number and ingredient list on any supplement label before use, and consult a registered medical practitioner before starting any supplement, especially if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, under 18, over 65, or taking prescription medication.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.step_of_action_if_violated: Remove the specific ingredient recommendation, route affected claim IDs through the regulatory_disallowed path in the content pipeline, and issue a public correction within 72 hours. Do not publish affiliate links to NMN sellers. For other ingredients, align dosage copy to FSSAI-permitted upper limits and re-verify the product FSSAI licence.

regulatory_regimes_in_scope.regime: CDSCO + ICMR-DBT National Guidelines for Stem Cell Research 2017 + New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules 2019
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.citation: Drugs and Cosmetics Act 1940; New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules 2019; ICMR-DBT National Guidelines for Stem Cell Research 2017
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.applicability_to_app: Bryan Johnson's protocol includes follistatin gene therapy, mesenchymal stem-cell infusions, total plasma exchange, cerebrolysin, and research peptides (BPC-157, TB-500, epithalon). These are not approved in India for longevity or anti-ageing indications. Peptides are unscheduled or unapproved under CDSCO. Stem-cell therapy for non-approved indications is expressly prohibited under the ICMR-DBT 2017 guidelines.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.required_disclaimer_text: The following interventions are described for context only and are NOT legal, NOT approved, and NOT recommended for use in India: follistatin gene therapy, mesenchymal stem-cell infusions for longevity, total plasma exchange for anti-ageing, cerebrolysin for non-approved indications, and research peptides including BPC-157, TB-500, and epithalon. Stem-cell therapy for ageing is expressly prohibited under ICMR-DBT National Guidelines for Stem Cell Research 2017 and the New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules 2019. We do not provide referrals, clinic lists, or purchase pathways for any of these. Do not self-source these from grey-market suppliers or overseas clinics; serious adverse events have been reported.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.step_of_action_if_violated: Immediately remove any content that could be read as a consumer pathway to these interventions, including clinic names, supplier links, or dosing instructions. Retain only educational context framed with the explicit non-recommendation. Legal review before any re-publication.

regulatory_regimes_in_scope.regime: Drugs and Magic Remedies (Objectionable Advertisements) Act 1954 + AYUSH advertising norms
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.citation: Drugs and Magic Remedies (Objectionable Advertisements) Act 1954 (DMRA); Ministry of AYUSH advisories on misleading claims; Rule 170 Drugs & Cosmetics Rules (as applicable)
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.applicability_to_app: The app describes ashwagandha, amla, turmeric, moringa, ghee, tulsi, and related botanicals, plus devices such as red-light caps, infrared saunas, and hyperbaric chambers. Presenting these as cures, treatments, or preventives of specified diseases (cancer, diabetes, heart disease, mental disorders, sexual conditions, etc. listed in the DMRA Schedule) is prohibited.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.required_disclaimer_text: No statement in this app should be read as a promise to cure, treat, mitigate, or prevent any disease listed under the Drugs and Magic Remedies (Objectionable Advertisements) Act, 1954, including diabetes, cancer, cardiac disorders, mental illness, sexual disorders, or any condition notified by the Ministry of AYUSH. Traditional herbs such as ashwagandha, amla, turmeric, moringa, and tulsi are described in the general-wellness and culinary context permitted under Indian food regulation; they are not medicines in the context of this app. If you have a diagnosed condition, please consult a registered medical practitioner or a registered AYUSH practitioner; do not self-treat on the basis of this app.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.step_of_action_if_violated: Scrub every piece of copy for DMRA-scheduled disease terms. Replace therapeutic verbs (cures, treats, prevents, reverses, heals) with wellness verbs (associated with, supports, may help maintain). Route any claim that cannot be reframed through the regulatory_disallowed path. Document the review and preserve for 3 years.

regulatory_regimes_in_scope.citation: Medical Devices Rules, 2017 (as amended); CDSCO device classification and registration notifications 2020-2024
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.applicability_to_app: The app references red-light caps (655 nm), infrared saunas, hyperbaric oxygen chambers, home BP monitors, continuous glucose monitors, smart scales, and body-composition and HRV devices. Any device marketed with a medical claim in India must be registered with CDSCO and carry a manufacturing or import licence number.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.required_disclaimer_text: Wearable devices, light therapy devices, saunas, hyperbaric chambers, blood-pressure monitors, and continuous glucose monitors described in this app are regulated under the Medical Devices Rules, 2017. Before purchase, verify that the product carries a valid CDSCO import or manufacturing licence number on the packaging. Readings from consumer wearables (HRV, sleep stage, body composition, single-lead ECG, CGM in non-diabetics) are indicative only and are not a substitute for clinical-grade diagnostic testing. Continuous glucose monitoring in non-diabetic users is not endorsed by Indian or international diabetes guidelines; do not self-diagnose prediabetes or diabetes from CGM traces, and discuss any persistent abnormality with a physician.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.step_of_action_if_violated: Restrict recommended-device lists to those with a verified CDSCO registration number captured in the verification record. Remove any device recommendation that cannot be tied to a licence number. Reframe any health benefit claims as lifestyle/comfort benefits rather than medical ones.

regulatory_regimes_in_scope.regime: Consumer Protection Act 2019 + Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) Guidelines for Prevention of Misleading Advertisements 2022
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.citation: Consumer Protection Act, 2019; CCPA Guidelines for Prevention of Misleading Advertisements and Endorsements for Misleading Advertisements, 2022
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.applicability_to_app: All price comparisons, product claims, biology claims, and mortality claims can be tested against the misleading-advertisement standard. The Devil's Advocate review documented (a) arithmetic error (Section C sums to Rs 3,062, not Rs 2,683; ratio must be ~1:11.6 for food-slice, not 1:1,770 for the whole protocol), (b) mustard-oil erucic-acid risk unmentioned, (c) '90% of measurable result' undefined and uncited.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.required_disclaimer_text: All price figures, availability, and comparison ratios are captured at the date shown at the top of each page and will vary with market conditions, vendor promotions, and regional availability. We re-verify pricing on a rolling basis but cannot guarantee real-time accuracy. Any saving, ratio, or percentage shown is a best-effort estimate for the specific basket compared; different baskets will give different results. Mortality and biomarker statistics quoted from published studies describe the study populations and are not individual outcome predictions; your personal risk depends on your genetics, medical history, and environment. Nothing on this app is a binding offer, guaranteed saving, or endorsement of a specific brand unless explicitly marked as an affiliate partnership.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.step_of_action_if_violated: Triage the specific advert, correct the copy within 48 hours, publish a dated correction note visible for at least 30 days, refund any paid affected user on request, and preserve the before/after copy for CCPA response.

regulatory_regimes_in_scope.citation: FSS (Contaminants, Toxins and Residues) Regulations 2011 (as amended); FSSAI heavy-metals limits for plant foods and supplements
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.applicability_to_app: Moringa, amla, turmeric, spirulina, and similar plant powders can accumulate Pb, Cd, and As from soil and water. Moringa is a known phytoremediator. Commercial powders vary widely in heavy-metal load. FSSAI sets limits for lead, cadmium, arsenic, and mercury in foods and supplements.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.required_disclaimer_text: Plant powders such as moringa, amla, turmeric, and spirulina can accumulate heavy metals (lead, cadmium, arsenic, mercury) from soil and water. Purchase only from brands that publish a recent Certificate of Analysis (COA) showing heavy-metal testing per FSSAI limits, or use fresh leaves from a known, clean source. Pregnant women, breastfeeding women, and children under 5 should not consume moringa leaf powder without explicit medical advice; moringa bark and root extracts are traditionally associated with uterine contractions and are not recommended in pregnancy.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.step_of_action_if_violated: Gate moringa, amla, turmeric, and spirulina recommendations behind a COA-checked brand whitelist. Remove any brand that fails or cannot produce a current COA. Add a pregnancy-warning overlay on the affected item pages.

regulatory_regimes_in_scope.regime: FSSAI Advertising and Claims Regulation 2018 (as amended) + Health-Claim substantiation norms
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.citation: FSS (Advertising and Claims) Regulations, 2018 and subsequent FSSAI health-claim guidance; FSSAI pre-approval requirement for disease-risk-reduction and function claims on food products
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.applicability_to_app: Claims such as 'reduces all-cause mortality by X percent', 'cocoa flavanols match Blueprint', 'jamun has higher anthocyanins than blueberries', 'mushrooms lower MCI risk', and 'daily legumes add years of life' are nutrient-function or disease-risk-reduction claims. For commercial promotion on food labels these require FSSAI substantiation; for editorial content they must be framed as study-population observations, not product promises.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.required_disclaimer_text: Mortality and health-benefit statistics quoted in this app come from published observational or randomised studies in specified populations (e.g., Finnish men, US Adventist vegetarians, Loma Linda cohort, Nurses' Health Study, Singapore Chinese adults, SPRINT high-cardiovascular-risk adults). These numbers describe the study population; they are not guaranteed outcomes for any individual Indian user. Indian South-Asian genetics (including FADS1/FADS2, OCTN1, LPA, and APOE variants), background diet, air and water quality, and healthcare access can all change the size of the benefit. We are not making a disease-risk-reduction claim about any specific food or brand.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.step_of_action_if_violated: Remove the specific percentage claim on the commercial surface; if the claim is editorial/contextual, retain with the study-population frame. Do not allow any branded vendor to display the claim adjacent to their product without their own FSSAI substantiation dossier.

regulatory_regimes_in_scope.regime: ASCI Code + ASCI Guidelines for Influencer Advertising in Digital Media 2021 (updated 2023)
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.citation: Advertising Standards Council of India Code; ASCI Guidelines for Influencer Advertising in Digital Media, 2021 (updated 2023); CCPA Endorsement Guidelines 2022
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.applicability_to_app: Any affiliate link to vendors (1mg, HealthKart, Amazon.in, Flipkart, BigBasket, Zepto, Blinkit, Ultrahuman, Oura, HealthifyMe, Thyrocare, Redcliffe, Apollo, Metropolis, SRL, TrueBasics, MuscleBlaze, Rasayanam, etc.), any paid review, any sponsored post on the app's social handles, and any creator collaboration must be labelled with a disclosure understandable to the average Indian consumer.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.required_disclaimer_text: Some product, supplement, lab, and device links on this app are affiliate links. If you click through and make a purchase, we may earn a commission at no additional cost to you. Affiliate relationships never change the editorial recommendation; products are added to the recommended list only after passing our verification checks on biology, price, and regulatory status. Every affiliate link is marked with an 'Ad' or 'Affiliate' tag alongside it, as required by the ASCI Guidelines for Influencer Advertising in Digital Media and the CCPA Endorsement Guidelines 2022.
regulatory_regimes_in_scope.step_of_action_if_violated: Audit every outbound link weekly. Remove the affiliate relationship if disclosure is inconsistent. Publish the full list of current affiliate partners on a permanent 'Transparency' page. Respond to ASCI or CCPA complaints within the stipulated timeline (typically 7 working days).

therapeutic_language_to_avoid: reduces all-cause mortality by X percent (as a personal promise)
adds X years to your life (as a personal promise)

permitted_reframes.permitted: in [study cohort, e.g., Finnish men aged 53, Laukkanen 2015], associated with up to 40% lower all-cause mortality; individual benefit in Indian populations is not established
traditionally used in Ayurveda for general wellness; some clinical studies report improvements in self-reported stress scores
NMN is a NAD+ precursor studied in early human trials; it is not approved as a nutraceutical ingredient under FSSAI and we do not recommend purchase

mustard oil provides monounsaturated fats; it contains 30-50% erucic acid and should be used in rotation with other oils. Adults with cardiovascular disease, elevated ApoB or LDL-C, or on lipid-lowering therapy should consult a physician before changing cooking oils

permitted_reframes.forbidden: walnuts give you the same omega-3 as fish oil
permitted_reframes.permitted: walnuts provide ALA (plant omega-3). Conversion of ALA to EPA and DHA in humans is limited (typically under 10% to EPA, under 1% to DHA) and may be further reduced in South Asians with FADS1/FADS2 variants. For marine omega-3 targets, algal or fish oil is still recommended

permitted_reframes.forbidden: moringa grows in every backyard, safe for everyone
permitted_reframes.permitted: moringa leaves are nutrient-dense; commercial moringa powder varies in heavy-metal content and should be purchased only with a recent Certificate of Analysis. Moringa root and bark extracts are traditionally contraindicated in pregnancy
ghee is approximately 62% saturated fat. Indian adults with elevated ApoB, LDL-C, or a family history of cardiovascular disease should discuss daily ghee intake with a physician

permitted_reframes.forbidden: sauna 4 times a week reduces cardiac mortality by 63%
permitted_reframes.permitted: a Finnish observational cohort of men (Laukkanen 2015) reported a 63% lower sudden cardiac death risk with 4-7 sauna sessions per week. Evidence in women, South Asians, and for infrared saunas is limited; sauna is contraindicated in uncontrolled hypertension, pregnancy, and certain cardiac conditions

permitted_reframes.forbidden: this stack replicates Bryan Johnson's longevity results
permitted_reframes.permitted: this stack approximates the food and daily-supplement portion of Bryan Johnson's protocol at Indian prices; it does not replicate his medical team, diagnostics, imaging, or experimental therapies, and does not guarantee any specific health outcome

continuous glucose monitors show glucose trends. In non-diabetic users, post-meal glucose spikes are often physiological; do not self-diagnose prediabetes from CGM data. Discuss persistent abnormal trends with a physician

required_disclaimers_on_every_page: General wellness information only, not medical advice. Not a substitute for a registered medical practitioner. Always consult a doctor before starting any supplement, exercise, fasting, sauna, cold-exposure, or light-therapy protocol, especially if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, under 18, over 65, or managing a diagnosed condition.
Prices shown were captured on the date at the top of the page; retail and quick-commerce prices change frequently.
Some outbound product links are affiliate links; see our Transparency page for the current affiliate list.
We do not sell, stock, dispense, or prescribe supplements, devices, or medical tests. All purchases are from third-party vendors subject to their own terms.

per_claim_disclosures.required_text: NMN has no approved nutraceutical status under FSSAI; products sold as NMN in India are legally ambiguous. We do not recommend purchase. The no-cost alternative of regular exercise plus niacin-rich dal is what we suggest for supporting NAD+ metabolism; tryptophan-from-dal to NAD+ conversion is inefficient and should not be read as equivalent to NMN supplementation.
NMN has no approved nutraceutical status under FSSAI; products sold in India as NMN are legally ambiguous. We do not recommend purchase until regulatory clarity exists.

Follistatin gene therapy, mesenchymal stem-cell infusions, total plasma exchange, cerebrolysin for non-approved indications, and research peptides (BPC-157, TB-500, epithalon) are described for context only and are NOT legal, NOT approved, and NOT recommended in India. Stem-cell therapy for ageing is prohibited under ICMR-DBT National Guidelines 2017. We do not provide clinic referrals, supplier links, or dosing guidance for any of these.

Mustard oil contains 30-50% erucic acid. Adults with cardiovascular disease, elevated ApoB/LDL-C, or those on lipid-lowering therapy should consult a physician before substituting mustard oil for their primary cooking fat. Consider rotating with low-erucic 'kachchi ghani' variants or sesame/groundnut oil. Mustard oil also lacks the olive-oil polyphenols (oleocanthal, hydroxytyrosol) that carry EU-recognised cardioprotective claims; this is a MUFA-for-MUFA swap only, not a polyphenol-for-polyphenol swap.
per_claim_disclosures.regime: Consumer Protection Act 2019 + FSSAI Advertising and Claims 2018

per_claim_disclosures.required_text: Mustard oil contains 30-50% erucic acid. Use in rotation; if you have cardiovascular disease or elevated ApoB/LDL-C, consult a physician before daily use.

Ghee is approximately 62% saturated fat. The A1/A2 casein distinction is about gut tolerance, not cardiovascular risk. If your LDL-C or ApoB is elevated, or if there is a family history of cardiovascular disease, keep ghee at flavour-only quantity (under 1 teaspoon per day) and discuss with your physician.
per_claim_disclosures.regime: FSSAI Advertising and Claims 2018 + Consumer Protection Act 2019

per_claim_disclosures.required_text: Moringa is a phytoremediator plant that can accumulate lead, cadmium, and arsenic from soil. Use only moringa powder brands that publish a current Certificate of Analysis within FSSAI heavy-metal limits, or use fresh leaves from a known-clean source. Not recommended in pregnancy; moringa bark/root extracts are traditionally associated with uterine contractions.

Commercial moringa powder varies widely in heavy-metal load (Pb, Cd, As) because moringa is a phytoremediator. Only brands with a recent COA within FSSAI limits are recommended. Pregnancy warning: avoid moringa leaf powder supplementation during pregnancy; moringa bark/root is contraindicated.

Turmeric is described as a culinary spice in this app, not as a medicine. Commercial turmeric powder has a history of lead contamination in certain regions; buy from brands that publish a COA. High-dose curcumin extracts have reported rare liver-injury cases; culinary doses are considered safe for most adults but not for those on blood thinners without physician advice.
Fresh amla is rich in vitamin C. Processed amla (candy, murabba, dried powder, chyawanprash) can lose 30-70% of vitamin C content; form matters. Vitamin C oral absorption plateaus around 200 mg/day.

Walnuts provide ALA (plant omega-3). Conversion of ALA to EPA/DHA in humans is limited (under 10% to EPA, under 1% to DHA) and may be further reduced in South Asians with FADS1/FADS2 variants. For cardioprotective marine-omega-3 targets, retain algal or fish oil in the stack; do not treat walnuts as a substitute for marine omega-3.

Ashwagandha (including KSM-66 standardised extract) is a traditional Ayurvedic herb used for general wellness. It is not a medicine in the context of this app and does not cure, treat, or prevent anxiety disorders, thyroid disease, or any condition listed under the Drugs and Magic Remedies Act 1954. Contraindicated in pregnancy, breastfeeding, with autoimmune conditions (Hashimoto's, lupus), and with sedative medications. Consult your doctor if you have thyroid disease.

KSM-66 is a standardised ashwagandha root extract. Clinical trials typically use 300-600 mg/day; verify that the product you buy states the standardised extract dose on the label. Not recommended in pregnancy, with autoimmune conditions, or with sedatives.

Indian raw-cacao brands (Ariba, Wakao, Mason and Co) do not publish product-specific flavanol COAs. We cannot confirm they match the flavanol content of Blueprint's cocoa. Treat these as non-alkalised cocoa with brand-dependent flavanol content; the FDA's 2023 qualified benchmark for cocoa-flavanol cardiovascular benefit is around 200 mg/day, which cannot be confirmed for any specific Indian brand without independent testing.
per_claim_disclosures.regime: FSSAI Advertising and Claims 2018 + Consumer Protection Act 2019

per_claim_disclosures.required_text: Only about 40% of humans efficiently convert pomegranate ellagitannins into urolithin A. Non-producers still receive general cardiometabolic benefits from the fruit but may not receive the mitophagy-related benefit. Producer status can shift with sustained intake. A direct urolithin-A supplement (e.g., Mitopure) bypasses this limitation.

Shiitake mushrooms actually contain more ergothioneine than oyster mushrooms (dry-weight basis). Ergothioneine figures cited in public literature are on dry weight; 50 g fresh mushroom (~90% water) delivers a fraction of the dry-weight amount. Retention also depends on OCTN1 transporter genotype. The Singapore mushroom-MCI study reported approximately 57% lower odds of MCI with 2 or more portions per week in ethnic Chinese adults; generalisation to Indian adults is unvalidated.

Jamun and blueberry anthocyanin comparisons in public literature are often reported on different weight bases (dry vs fresh); absorbed anthocyanin in humans is under 1% of intake for both. Jamun is seasonal (typically May-July); off-season, frozen blueberries (well-preserved anthocyanins) or dried jamun powder (different profile) are alternatives.
Data from Doll and Peto and Indian cohorts (Gupta et al. Mumbai) support substantial mortality reduction on cessation; the '~50%' figure is a cohort-level estimate, not an individual guarantee.

RCT meta-analyses support roughly 15-20% all-cause mortality reduction from sustained weight control; observational studies suggesting larger effects are confounded. In South Asian adults, waist-to-height ratio is a better risk marker than BMI because of the 'thin-fat' phenotype.

Mortality benefit of physical activity follows a dose-response curve. At the guideline-minimum 150 minutes/week of moderate activity, the benefit is approximately 20-25%; the ~35% figure reflects the high-activity tier. Occupational and transport-related activity (walking, cycling) count.

Intensive BP control data (SPRINT) excluded people with diabetes, prior stroke, or significant frailty, and the ~30% benefit does not directly transfer to those groups. Intensive BP control in the elderly carries risks of syncope, falls, and acute kidney injury. Discuss targets with a physician.

Mediterranean-style eating is associated with approximately 21% lower all-cause mortality in meta-analyses. A refined-grain, dairy-heavy Indian vegetarian diet is not automatically Mediterranean; benefit depends on oil choice, whole-grain substitution, regular legumes, and limited dairy fat.

The Laukkanen 2015 Finnish cohort is 2,315 men aged ~53 using traditional Finnish dry sauna. Generalisation to Indian women, infrared saunas, and hot-climate users is unvalidated. Sauna is contraindicated in uncontrolled hypertension, pregnancy, and several cardiac conditions; hydrate aggressively in Indian climate.
See the free5_sauna_finnish disclosure. Do not extrapolate the 40% all-cause or 63% sudden-cardiac-death figures to yourself without physician input.

The '+2 to 3 years lifespan' from nut consumption is a modelled projection from the Adventist Health Study-2, not a direct observation; Adventists are a self-selected low-alcohol, low-smoking, health-conscious cohort, so healthy-user confounding is substantial.

Meta-analytic estimate is approximately 6% lower all-cause mortality per 50 g cooked legume per day, with a plateau beyond ~150 g/day. Typical North-Indian dal intake may already be at or beyond the plateau; additional servings are not linearly beneficial.

The 1:1,770 ratio is between Bryan Johnson's total annual longevity spend (which includes his medical team, experimental therapies, diagnostics, and equipment) and the Indian food-plus-daily-supplements alternative. The Indian alternative does NOT include labs, imaging, epigenetic clocks, hyperbaric oxygen, plasma exchange, peptides, or a medical team. The food-and-supplement slice alone is ~1:11.6 at corrected arithmetic. We cannot and do not claim '90% of measurable result' for the Indian alternative; that phrase has been retired from the app.

The daily food-and-supplement replica cost is based on the sum of items in the Blueprint-replica basket. The corrected sum as of this capture date is Rs 3,062/day; the earlier Rs 2,683 figure contained an arithmetic error and has been withdrawn.

Red-light therapy devices marketed with medical claims (hair regrowth, collagen, mitochondrial function) fall under CDSCO Medical Devices Rules 2017. Buy only devices with a valid CDSCO import or manufacturing licence number. Treat the benefit as cosmetic unless a CDSCO-registered clinical claim applies to the specific device.

Hyperbaric oxygen therapy is a CDSCO-regulated medical procedure in India. Home hyperbaric chambers for wellness use are unapproved for medical indications. We do not recommend home HBOT for longevity; clinic-based HBOT should be undertaken only on the advice of, and under the supervision of, a qualified physician.

Infrared saunas are regulated as consumer wellness devices in India. Health claims (detoxification, cardiovascular benefit) require CDSCO clearance. Contraindicated in uncontrolled hypertension, pregnancy, several cardiac conditions, and in heat-susceptible individuals.

Continuous glucose monitors (Abbott FreeStyle Libre, Ultrahuman M1, Dexcom) are CDSCO-regulated medical devices. Use in non-diabetic adults is not endorsed by the ADA, Endocrine Society, or ICMR as a consensus practice. Post-meal glucose spikes in non-diabetic users are often physiological; do not self-diagnose prediabetes.

HRV and sleep-stage readings from consumer wearables are indicative, not diagnostic. Oura Gen3 has more peer-reviewed validation than Ultrahuman Ring Air; Ring Air is a promising Indian option but validation relative to polysomnography is thinner.

Use only CDSCO-registered home BP monitors (Omron, Dr Morepen, BPL, etc. with valid licence numbers). Calibrate annually. If readings are persistently above 140/90, consult a physician; do not self-medicate.

DunedinPACE epigenetic ageing tests are currently offered by overseas providers (TruDiagnostic and similar) and may require international shipping of a buccal/blood sample. Turnaround, regulatory status of sample export under Indian rules, and clinical actionability of the result are all unsettled. Treat as a research-grade measurement.
Horvath epigenetic-clock services in India have similar cross-border sampling and clinical-actionability limits as DunedinPACE. Not a diagnostic test for any disease.

Lab panels linked in this app are provided by third-party laboratories (Thyrocare, Redcliffe, 1mg Labs, Metropolis, SRL, Apollo). Panel contents and prices change; verify with the lab before booking. Lab results should be interpreted by a registered medical practitioner.

coa_requirements.required_coa: heavy metals Pb/Cd/As per FSSAI limits for plant foods; microbial load; pesticide residues

coa_requirements.category: commercial amla powder / dried amla / murabba
coa_requirements.required_coa: heavy metals Pb/Cd/As per FSSAI limits; vitamin C assay (per 100 g) for any product claiming 'high vitamin C'
lead below FSSAI limit (regionally flagged for lead contamination); curcumin percentage if any potency claim is made

coa_requirements.category: raw Indian cacao brands (Ariba, Wakao, Mason and Co)
coa_requirements.required_coa: flavanol content per serving (mg); cadmium per FSSAI limits; alkalisation status (Dutched or non-Dutched)
withanolide content; standardised extract mg per capsule; heavy metals; absence of adulterant species
erucic acid percentage; FSSAI licence; free fatty acid levels; absence of argemone oil adulteration
EPA and DHA mg per serving; oxidation markers (TOTOX); heavy metals; FSSAI licence
polyphenol content (hydroxytyrosol/oleocanthal); free acidity; peroxide value; country/region of origin
full heavy-metals panel; microbial load; ingredient identity (DNA or HPLC) for any botanical
heavy metals per FSSAI; protein percentage; amino-acid profile; FSSAI licence
each active at label dose; FSSAI licence; NMN content if declared (flag as FSSAI-ambiguous)

affiliate_disclosure_template: This page may contain affiliate links. If you click through and make a purchase, we may earn a commission at no additional cost to you. Affiliate relationships do not change which products we recommend; a product is added to the recommended list only after passing our verification checks on biology, regulatory status, and price. Per the ASCI Guidelines for Influencer Advertising in Digital Media (2021, updated 2023) and the CCPA Endorsement Guidelines 2022, every affiliate link is tagged 'Ad' or 'Affiliate' adjacent to the link. A full list of current affiliate partners is published on our Transparency page. Readers are free to buy any recommended product from any vendor of their choice.

user_rights_and_refund.consumer_protection_act_2019: Users have rights under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019 including the right to be informed, the right to choose, the right to be heard, and the right to redressal. Complaints about misleading content on this app can be filed with the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) and with consumer forums at district, state, and national level.
user_rights_and_refund.grievance_officer: We appoint a Grievance Officer per the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021. Contact details for the Grievance Officer are published on the app's Contact page; complaints are acknowledged within 24 hours and resolved within 15 days.
user_rights_and_refund.data_protection: User data is handled per the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023. Users can request access, correction, or deletion of their personal data by writing to the Grievance Officer.
user_rights_and_refund.refund_policy: The app itself is free / operates on a freemium subscription model; subscription refunds are available pro-rata within 7 days of payment, except where the user has already consumed gated premium content (lab booking concierge, 1:1 consults). Third-party purchases (supplements, devices, lab tests) are subject to the selling vendor's refund policy; we do not process those refunds but will facilitate vendor contact.
user_rights_and_refund.medical_emergency: This app is not for medical emergencies. If you believe you are experiencing a medical emergency (chest pain, stroke symptoms, severe allergic reaction, suicidal ideation), call 112 (India emergency) or iCall (9152987821) immediately.
user_rights_and_refund.reporting_a_misleading_claim: If you believe any statement on this app is misleading, inaccurate, or potentially in violation of FSSAI, CDSCO, AYUSH, DMRA, ASCI, or CCPA norms, email the Grievance Officer. We commit to public correction within 72 hours of verification and preservation of the correction notice for a minimum of 30 days.
